agent_context: Assistant that maintains a shared team playbook.

existing.content: Always include a short summary at the top of a generated report.
existing.trigger: generating a report for the user
existing.rationale: Readers skim the summary before deciding to read on.

candidate.content: Always add a brief summary at the start of any report you produce.
candidate.trigger: generating a report for the user
candidate.rationale: A leading summary helps the reader orient quickly.

notes: Classic dedup: the candidate restates the existing same-trigger rule almost verbatim. Unify (absorb the existing) with no self-contradiction.

agent_context: Assistant that drafts outbound customer emails.

existing.content: Always greet the recipient by name in the email opening.
existing.rationale: A personal greeting improves the open and reply rate.

candidate.content: Do not attach internal-only files to a customer email.
candidate.rationale: Internal files can leak confidential data to the customer.

notes: Compose case: existing is a do-rule about the greeting; candidate is an avoid-rule about a DIFFERENT sub-aspect (attachments) of the SAME task (drafting a customer email). Unify into one multi-rule skill holding both the do-rule and the avoid-rule. No self-contradiction: they govern distinct sub-aspects, not the same situation.

agent_context: Assistant that decides how to respond to incoming requests.

existing.content: Always answer the question immediately without asking for confirmation.
existing.trigger: the user asks a factual question
existing.rationale: Fast answers keep the conversation moving.

candidate.content: Do not answer immediately; ask a clarifying question first.
candidate.trigger: the user asks a factual question
candidate.rationale: Some questions are ambiguous and answering blindly misleads the user.

notes: Linchpin: existing is positive and candidate is negative on the SAME trigger/situation (opposite advice). These must NOT be unified into one row (that would be a self-contradiction). Differentiate: refine both triggers so the contexts no longer overlap.

existing.content: Always run the full test suite and the linter before approving a change.
existing.rationale: Catches regressions and style violations before merge.

candidate.content: Always run the tests before approving a change.

notes: The candidate is strictly weaker than the existing rule (tests only, no linter) on the same trigger. The existing rule supersedes it. Reject the candidate as redundant (a storage no-op).

existing.content: Always propose three meeting time options when scheduling.
existing.trigger: scheduling a meeting between two people

candidate.content: Use metric units when reporting measurements.
candidate.trigger: reporting a physical measurement to the user
candidate.rationale: The user's region uses the metric system.

notes: The candidate (measurement units) is unrelated to any existing row (meeting scheduling). Insert it as-is, independent of the existing rule.
